exit_criteria: User requirement captured as approved session-control scope change.
Pi docs evidence identifies command-context ctx.newSession as adapter mechanism.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Agency and compiler routing can request a new/fresh session or context reset when policy requires it without asking the user to do it manually.
Pi adapter should expose session replacement using Pi's command-context newSession API, because tools cannot call it directly.
Session-control contract should be adapter-neutral so other harnesses can implement their own clear-context/session-spawn mechanism.
source_refs: user: other harnesses need different clearing mechanisms

summary: Accepted scope change: CodeWiki agency should be able to request fresh sessions/context windows itself, without asking the user to manually run /new, using adapter-specific session handoff support.

diff_table.current_state: Isolation policy tells agents to start fresh, but cannot trigger new session/context itself.
diff_table.desired_state: CodeWiki exposes an adapter session handoff action that creates/queues a fresh session with minimal kickoff context.
diff_table.rationale: Agency cannot autonomously work across sprints if user must manually run /new at each boundary.

diff_table.current_state: Pi has /new and command-context ctx.newSession(), but CodeWiki has no LLM-callable handoff tool.
diff_table.desired_state: Pi CodeWiki adapter provides a tool/command bridge: tool stages handoff, command performs ctx.newSession(), fresh session receives kickoff prompt.
diff_table.rationale: Pi tools cannot directly replace sessions; docs recommend command bridge patterns for reload/session control.

diff_table.current_state: No durable adapter-neutral session handoff semantics exist in CodeWiki knowledge.
diff_table.desired_state: Knowledge/API define session handoff as capability with mode new-session/context-reset/external-orchestrator, isolation evidence, and fallback behavior.
diff_table.rationale: Other harnesses can map same semantic request to their own session APIs.

accepted_decisions.summary: Automatic session handoff is now in scope.
Pi adapter should use ctx.newSession from a command, not ask the user to run /new.
The LLM-facing tool should stage/queue a handoff and report that the current session will be replaced.

assumptions: Pi extension commands can safely call ctx.newSession and then withSession.sendUserMessage from docs.
Tool-to-command bridge is acceptable for tools because tool contexts cannot call ctx.newSession directly.
non_goals: Implement every non-Pi harness in this task.
Build a full sprint scheduler if a bounded session-handoff primitive is sufficient.
